title: selinum repens
intro: Selinum repens, also known as wild carrot or Queen Anne's lace, is a biennial plant that belongs to the parsley family. It is commonly found in Europe, Asia, and North Africa, and is known for its medicinal properties, which include its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Selinum repens include its ability to relieve digestive issues, reduce pain and inflammation, and support the immune system. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and can be consumed fresh or dried to reap its medicinal benefits.
